description: A simple, clean designed line-chart infographic on a white background titled in bold black text 'Federal Employment.' A single dark navy-blue line traces the size of the federal workforce month by month across the x-axis (labeled diagonally 'January 2025' through 'November 2025'), with a y-axis ranging from 2,750,000 up to 3,050,000. The line starts just above 3,000,000 in January and declines steadily, then drops sharply between September and October 2025 to about 2,750,000, where it flattens. The chart is minimalist with light-gray horizontal gridlines and no logos or photos. It is a designed data visualization used as propaganda to dramatize the administration's claim of shrinking the federal workforce (the tweet boasts that the federal workforce is decreasing while job growth goes to American-born workers). The steep cliff emphasizes the cuts. Target audience: fiscal conservatives, supporters of government downsizing, and the general public.
